words.definitionEn: a university or college student who is studying for their first degree
words.exampleEn: a first-year undergraduate

words.definitionEn: important in a situation but not always easily noticed or stated clearly
words.exampleEn: The underlying assumption is that the amount of money available is limited.

words.definitionEn: to make something, especially somebody’s confidence or authority, gradually weaker or less effective
words.exampleEn: Our confidence in the team has been seriously undermined by their recent defeats.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that something exists or is definitely true
words.exampleEn: There is undoubtedly a great deal of truth in what he says.

words.definitionEn: very strange or unusual
words.exampleEn: a bizarre situation/incident/story

words.definitionEn: to join people, things, parts of a country, etc. together so that they form a single unit
words.exampleEn: The new leader hopes to unify the country.

words.definitionEn: that has never happened, been done or been known before
words.exampleEn: The situation is unprecedented in modern times.

words.definitionEn: to remove a cover or curtain from a painting, statue, etc. so that it can be seen in public for the first time
words.exampleEn: The Queen unveiled a plaque to mark the official opening of the hospital.

words.definitionEn: going to happen soon
words.exampleEn: the upcoming presidential election

words.definitionEn: the flat part of a knife, tool or machine, which has a sharp edge or edges for cutting
words.exampleEn: The machine comes with a plastic guard over the blade to protect the operator.

words.definitionEn: the act of making a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; the more powerful and efficient machine, computer system, etc.
words.exampleEn: instructions for installing an upgrade to the existing system

words.definitionEn: to make a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; to start using a new and better version of a machine, system, etc.
words.exampleEn: upgrade something, We are constantly upgrading our software to meet customers' needs.

words.definitionEn: to support something that you think is right and make sure that it continues to exist
words.exampleEn: We have a duty to uphold the law.

words.definitionEn: a service provided for the public, for example an electricity, water or gas supply
words.exampleEn: a privatized electricity utility

words.definitionEn: to use something, especially for a practical purpose
words.exampleEn: The Romans were the first to utilize concrete as a building material.

words.definitionEn: completely
words.exampleEn: We're so utterly different from each other.

words.definitionEn: a space that is completely empty of all substances, including all air or other gas
words.exampleEn: a vacuum pump (= one that creates a vacuum)

words.definitionEn: not clear in a person’s mind
words.exampleEn: to have a vague impression/memory/recollection of something

words.definitionEn: the state of being legally or officially acceptable
words.exampleEn: The period of validity of the agreement has expired.

words.definitionEn: to disappear suddenly and/or in a way that you cannot explain
words.exampleEn: He turned around and vanished into the house.

words.definitionEn: often changing; likely to change
words.exampleEn: variable temperatures

words.definitionEn: a situation, number or quantity that can vary or be varied
words.exampleEn: With so many variables, it is difficult to calculate the cost.

words.definitionEn: of many different types
words.exampleEn: varied opinions

words.definitionEn: any of the tubes that carry blood from all parts of the body towards the heart
words.exampleEn: the jugular vein

words.definitionEn: a business project or activity, especially one that involves taking risks
words.exampleEn: A disastrous business venture lost him thousands of dollars.

words.definitionEn: to go somewhere even though you know that it might be dangerous or unpleasant
words.exampleEn: They ventured nervously into the water.

words.definitionEn: relating to words
words.exampleEn: The job applicant must have good verbal skills.

words.definitionEn: an official judgement made in court or at an inquest (= an official investigation into somebody's death)
words.exampleEn: Has the jury reached a verdict?

words.definitionEn: to check that something is true or accurate
words.exampleEn: verify something, We have no way of verifying his story.

words.definitionEn: writing that is arranged in lines, often with a regular rhythm or pattern of rhyme
words.exampleEn: in verse, Most of the play is written in verse, but some of it is in prose.

words.definitionEn: used to show that two teams or sides are against each other
words.exampleEn: It is France versus Brazil in the final.

words.definitionEn: an explosion or a powerful movement of air caused by an explosion
words.exampleEn: a bomb blast

words.definitionEn: a tube that carries blood through the body of a person or an animal, or liquid through the parts of a plant
words.exampleEn: to burst/rupture a blood vessel

words.definitionEn: a person who has a lot of experience in a particular area or activity
words.exampleEn: the veteran American actor, Clint Eastwood

words.definitionEn: that can be done; that will be successful
words.exampleEn: a viable option/proposition

words.definitionEn: full of life and energy
words.exampleEn: a vibrant city

words.definitionEn: criminal activities that involve sex or drugs
words.exampleEn: At the door were two plain-clothes detectives from the vice squad.

words.definitionEn: violent and cruel
words.exampleEn: a vicious attack

words.definitionEn: to violently destroy or break something into pieces, using explosives
words.exampleEn: They blasted a huge crater in the runway.

words.definitionEn: a person who lives in a village
words.exampleEn: Some of the villagers have lived here all their lives.

words.definitionEn: to go against or refuse to obey a law, an agreement, etc.
words.exampleEn: to violate international law

words.definitionEn: that contains acid or has the essential characteristics of an acid; that has a pH of less than seven
words.exampleEn: Rye is tolerant of poor, acid soils.

words.definitionEn: to lose blood, especially from a wound or an injury
words.exampleEn: My finger's bleeding.

words.definitionEn: the act of going against or refusing to obey a law, an agreement, etc.
words.exampleEn: They were in open violation of the treaty.

words.definitionEn: behaviour or attitudes that show high moral standards
words.exampleEn: He led a life of virtue.

words.definitionEn: a mixture of different types of the same thing
words.exampleEn: a blend of tea

words.definitionEn: connected with the voice
words.exampleEn: vocal music

words.definitionEn: to mix two or more substances together
words.exampleEn: blend A with B, Blend the flour with the milk to make a smooth paste.

words.definitionEn: to make a formal and serious promise to do something or a formal statement that is true
words.exampleEn: vow to do something, She vowed never to speak to him again.

words.definitionEn: the fact of being weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
words.exampleEn: the vulnerability of newborn babies to disease

words.definitionEn: weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
words.exampleEn: These offices are highly vulnerable to terrorist attack.

words.definitionEn: to ask God to protect somebody/something
words.exampleEn: They brought the children to Jesus and he blessed them.

words.definitionEn: a separate room or area in a hospital for people with the same type of medical condition
words.exampleEn: a maternity/surgical/psychiatric/children’s ward

words.definitionEn: a building where large quantities of goods are stored, especially before they are sent to shops to be sold
words.exampleEn: Police are investigating a fire at a furniture warehouse.

words.definitionEn: God’s help and protection, or a prayer asking for this
words.exampleEn: to pray for God’s blessing

words.definitionEn: the activity of fighting a war, especially using particular weapons or methods
words.exampleEn: air/naval/guerrilla warfare

words.definitionEn: a legal document that is signed by a judge and gives the police authority to do something
words.exampleEn: an arrest warrant

words.definitionEn: to make something necessary or appropriate in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: warrant something, Further investigation is clearly warranted.

words.definitionEn: (especially in the past) a person who fights in a battle or war
words.exampleEn: a warrior nation (= whose people are skilled in fighting)

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/something less strong or powerful; to become less strong or powerful
words.exampleEn: The team has been weakened by injury.

words.definitionEn: to make cloth, a carpet, a basket, etc. by crossing threads or narrow pieces of material across, over and under each other by hand or on a machine called a loom
words.exampleEn: weave A from B, The baskets are woven from strips of willow.

words.definitionEn: a wild plant growing where it is not wanted, especially among crops or garden plants
words.exampleEn: The yard was overgrown with weeds.

words.definitionEn: a deep hole in the ground from which people obtain water. The sides of wells are usually covered with brick or stone and there is usually a cover or a small wall at the top of the well.
words.exampleEn: to dig/sink a well

words.definitionEn: general health and happiness
words.exampleEn: emotional/physical/psychological well-being

words.definitionEn: not at all; not of any kind
words.exampleEn: They received no help whatever.

words.definitionEn: not at all; not of any kind
words.exampleEn: They received no help whatsoever.

words.definitionEn: by which; because of which
words.exampleEn: They have introduced a new system whereby all employees must undergo regular training.

words.definitionEn: during the time that something is happening; at the same time as something else is happening
words.exampleEn: In the UK it is illegal to drive whilst holding a mobile phone.

words.definitionEn: to hit a person or an animal hard with a whip, as a punishment or to make them go faster or work harder
words.exampleEn: He was taken back to the jail and soundly whipped.

words.definitionEn: completely
words.exampleEn: wholly inappropriate behaviour

words.definitionEn: to become wider; to make something wider
words.exampleEn: Her eyes widened in surprise.

words.definitionEn: a woman whose husband or wife has died and who has not married again
words.exampleEn: She gets a widow’s pension.

words.definitionEn: the measurement from one side of something to the other; how wide something is
words.exampleEn: The terrace runs the full width of the house.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being happy and ready to do something
words.exampleEn: Success in studying depends on a willingness to learn.

words.definitionEn: to rub something against a surface, in order to remove dirt or liquid from it; to rub a surface with a cloth, etc. in order to clean it
words.exampleEn: wipe something (on something), Please wipe your feet on the mat.

words.definitionEn: the ability to say or write things that are both clever and humorous
words.exampleEn: to have a quick/sharp/dry/ready wit

words.definitionEn: the act of taking an amount of money out of your bank account
words.exampleEn: You can make withdrawals of up to $250 a day.

words.definitionEn: to talk in a way that shows you are too proud of something that you have or can do
words.exampleEn: I don't want to boast, but I can actually speak six languages.

words.definitionEn: a period of physical exercise that you do to keep fit
words.exampleEn: She does a 20-minute workout every morning.

words.definitionEn: the practice of showing respect for God or a god, by saying prayers, singing with others, etc.; a ceremony for this
words.exampleEn: an act/a place of worship

words.definitionEn: to show respect for God or a god, especially by saying prayers, singing, etc. with other people in a religious building
words.exampleEn: The Mayans built jungle pyramids to worship their gods.

words.definitionEn: important, pleasant, interesting, etc.; worth spending time, money or effort on
words.exampleEn: It was in aid of a worthwhile cause (= a charity, etc.).

words.definitionEn: having the qualities that deserve somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Very few of his ideas are worthy of further attention.

words.definitionEn: to shout loudly, for example because you are angry, excited, frightened or in pain
words.exampleEn: yell (at somebody/something), He yelled at the other driver.

words.definitionEn: the total amount of crops, profits, etc. that are produced
words.exampleEn: a high crop yield

words.definitionEn: the total amount of crops, profits, etc. that are produced
words.exampleEn: a high crop yield

words.definitionEn: a young person or a child
words.exampleEn: The camp is for youngsters aged 8 to 14.

words.definitionEn: an extra amount of money that is added to a payment, especially to somebody’s wages or salary as a reward
words.exampleEn: a £100 Christmas bonus

words.definitionEn: a sudden increase in trade and economic activity; a period of wealth and success
words.exampleEn: Living standards improved rapidly during the post-war boom.

words.definitionEn: the act of getting something, especially knowledge, a skill, etc.
words.exampleEn: theories of child language acquisition

words.definitionEn: if something bounces or you bounce it, it moves quickly away from a surface it has just hit or you make it do this
words.exampleEn: The ball bounced twice before he could reach it.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring an area of land; 4 840 square yards or about 4 050 square metres
words.exampleEn: 3 000 acres of parkland

words.definitionEn: a real or imagined line that marks the limits or edges of something and separates it from other things or places; a dividing line
words.exampleEn: After the war the national boundaries were redrawn.

words.definitionEn: the act of bending your head or the upper part of your body forward in order to say hello or goodbye to somebody or to show respect
words.exampleEn: She gave a slight bow of her head in greeting.

words.definitionEn: to move your head or the top half of your body forwards and downwards as a sign of respect or to say hello or goodbye
words.exampleEn: bow (to/before somebody/something), He bowed low to the assembled crowd.

words.definitionEn: a failure to do something that must be done by law
words.exampleEn: a breach of contract/copyright/warranty

words.definitionEn: to not keep to an agreement or not keep a promise
words.exampleEn: The government is accused of breaching the terms of the treaty.

words.definitionEn: an occasion when a vehicle or machine stops working
words.exampleEn: a breakdown on the motorway

words.definitionEn: an important development that may lead to an agreement or achievement
words.exampleEn: to make/achieve a breakthrough

words.definitionEn: a particular type of animal that has been developed by people in a certain way, especially a type of dog, cat or farm animal
words.exampleEn: Labradors and other large breeds of dog

words.definitionEn: to have sex and produce young
words.exampleEn: Many animals breed only at certain times of the year.

words.definitionEn: a way of connecting to the internet that allows you to receive information, including pictures, etc., very quickly and that is always active (so that the user does not have to connect each time)
words.exampleEn: plans to provide rural areas with fast broadband

words.definitionEn: a computer program that lets you look at or read documents on the World Wide Web
words.exampleEn: What do you use as your default browser?

words.definitionEn: violent and cruel
words.exampleEn: a brutal attack/murder/rape/killing

words.definitionEn: a US, Australian or New Zealand dollar; a South African rand; an Indian rupee
words.exampleEn: They cost ten bucks.

words.definitionEn: a friend
words.exampleEn: an old college buddy of mine

words.definitionEn: a thing or person that reduces a shock or protects somebody/something against difficulties
words.exampleEn: buffer against something, Support from family and friends acts as a buffer against stress.

words.definitionEn: the main part of something; most of something
words.exampleEn: The bulk of the population lives in cities.

words.definitionEn: a duty, responsibility, etc. that causes worry, difficulty or hard work
words.exampleEn: to bear/carry/ease/reduce/share the burden

words.definitionEn: the system of official rules and ways of doing things that a government or an organization has, especially when these seem to be too complicated
words.exampleEn: unnecessary/excessive bureaucracy

words.definitionEn: the act or ceremony of burying a dead body
words.exampleEn: a burial place/mound/site

words.definitionEn: to break open or apart, especially because of pressure from inside; to make something break in this way
words.exampleEn: That balloon will burst if you blow it up any more.

words.definitionEn: the act of making something such as a device or chemical process start working
words.exampleEn: The activation of several target genes results in two major effects.

words.definitionEn: a group of senior members of a government that is responsible for advising and deciding on government policy
words.exampleEn: a cabinet meeting

words.definitionEn: the act or process of using numbers to find out an amount
words.exampleEn: Cathy did a rough calculation.

words.definitionEn: a person who works to achieve political or social change, especially as a member of an organization with particular aims
words.exampleEn: human/civil/animal rights activists

words.definitionEn: a strong heavy rough material used for making tents, sails, etc. and by artists for painting on
words.exampleEn: tents made from heavy canvas

words.definitionEn: the ability or qualities necessary to do something
words.exampleEn: Age affects the range of a person's capabilities.

words.definitionEn: an economic system in which a country’s businesses and industry are controlled and run for profit by private owners rather than by the government
words.exampleEn: the growth of industrial capitalism in the West

words.definitionEn: based on the principles of capitalism
words.exampleEn: a capitalist society/system/economy

words.definitionEn: the goods carried in a ship, an aircraft or a motor vehicle
words.exampleEn: The tanker began to spill its cargo of oil.

words.definitionEn: a separate section of a train for carrying passengers
words.exampleEn: a railway carriage

words.definitionEn: to make objects, patterns, etc. by cutting away material from a piece of wood or stone, or another hard material
words.exampleEn: carve something, a carved doorway

words.definitionEn: a public building or room where people play gambling games for money
words.exampleEn: a new hotel and casino in Las Vegas

words.definitionEn: a person who is killed or injured in war or in an accident
words.exampleEn: Our primary objective is reducing road casualties.

words.definitionEn: a complete list of items, for example of things that people can look at or buy
words.exampleEn: a mail-order catalogue (= a book showing goods for sale to be sent to people’s homes)

words.definitionEn: to provide food and drinks for a social event
words.exampleEn: (British English), cater for somebody/something, Most of our work now involves catering for weddings.

words.definitionEn: cows and bulls that are kept as farm animals for their milk or meat
words.exampleEn: a herd of cattle

words.definitionEn: very serious or severe
words.exampleEn: There is an acute shortage of water.

words.definitionEn: care that you take in order to avoid danger or mistakes; the fact of not taking any risks
words.exampleEn: extreme/great caution

words.definitionEn: being careful about what you say or do, especially to avoid danger or mistakes; not taking any risks
words.exampleEn: The government has been cautious in its response to the report.

words.definitionEn: to stop happening or existing; to stop something from happening or existing
words.exampleEn: Welfare payments cease as soon as an individual starts a job.

words.definitionEn: an area of land used for burying dead people, especially one that is not next to a church
words.exampleEn: He was buried in a private cemetery.

words.definitionEn: the action or process of changing something, or of being changed, to suit a new purpose or situation
words.exampleEn: the adaptation of buildings for military purposes

words.definitionEn: a hall in a public building that is used for formal meetings
words.exampleEn: The members left the council chamber.
